title: API: Unable to remove image throws 500 error
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. start podman service

2. try to remove an image via the api which does have more than one tag

**Describe the results you received:**
```
   {"cause":"unable to match user input to any specific repotag",
    "message":"unable to match user input to any specific repotag",
    "response":500} 
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
404 or other 4xx error

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.8
Built:        Fri Feb 19 16:56:17 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
in addition, to a wrong input it returns a http 500 error containing a 404 and 500 json error in the body

e.g.
```
curl -v -X DELETE "http://127.0.0.1:2375/images/"
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:2375...
* Connected to 127.0.0.1 (127.0.0.1) port 2375 (#0)
> DELETE /images/ HTTP/1.1
> Host: 127.0.0.1:2375
> User-Agent: curl/7.71.1
> Accept: */*
> 
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error
< Api-Version: 1.40
< Content-Type: application/json
< Libpod-Api-Version: 3.0.0
< Server: Libpod/3.0.0 (linux)
< Date: Wed, 03 Mar 2021 15:00:16 GMT
< Content-Length: 204
< 
{"cause":"input name is blank","message":"failed to find image : input name is blank","response":500}
{"cause":"input name is blank","message":"failed to find image : input name is blank","response":404}
```
Likely a missing return statement somewhere...

Looking at this, I'm fairly certain this isn't actually hitting the remove images handler - I think that, since the URL isn't really valid (missing a name), some other handling at a lower level is throwing the error.

@jwhonce PTAL
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@mheon, @zyclonite: The code on the master branch now handles this error much cleaner. 

@zyclonite: @mheon is correct that there is no defined RESTful endpoint `DELETE:/images/`. Most examples of not providing a path parameter would result in a 404 status code, but you found a corner case where the gorilla routing mux code passed your call to the the defined endpoint `DELETE:/images/{name}` with an empty `name` parameter. See results below.

$ curl -v -X DELETE --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock http://localhost/images/
* Uses proxy env variable no_proxy == 'localhost,127.0.0.0/8,.local,192.168.250.53/24'
*   Trying /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock:0...
* Connected to localhost (/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock) port 80 (#0)
> DELETE /images/ HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost
> User-Agent: curl/7.71.1
> Accept: */*
> 
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error
< Api-Version: 1.40
< Content-Type: application/json
< Libpod-Api-Version: 3.2.0-dev
< Server: Libpod/3.2.0-dev (linux)
< Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2021 03:24:43 GMT
< Content-Length: 79
< 
{"cause":"input name is blank","message":"input name is blank","response":500}
